result.name: STL Batch Validator (20-file supplier batch)
result.description: Validates a batch of ASCII/binary STL files for manufacturing suitability: checks manifold integrity (open/non-manifold edges), degenerate triangles, duplicate faces, and inverted normals (declared vs winding order). Fixes the hidden failure mode in the provided validator. Writes results to validation_report.json.

result.name: Binary & ASCII STL Validator
result.description: Validates both binary and ASCII STL files for manufacturing suitability: detects format automatically, checks manifold integrity (open/non-manifold edges), degenerate triangles, duplicate faces, inverted normals (declared vs winding order), and binary file size consistency.

result.name: STL Validator CLI (ASCII + Binary)
result.description: CLI tool accepting a single STL file path and printing JSON {valid, issues}. Handles both ASCII and binary STL. Checks manifold integrity, degenerate triangles, duplicate faces, binary file-size consistency, and inverted normals (declared vs winding order). Fixes the hidden failure mode in the provided validator.

result.name: Improved STL Validator
result.description: Validates ASCII STL files for manifold integrity, open edges, degenerate triangles, duplicate faces, and — unlike the provided validator — correctly checks that declared face normals are consistent with vertex winding order.

result.name: Improved STL Validator v2 (ASCII + Binary)
result.description: Validates ASCII and binary STL files for manufacturing suitability: checks manifold integrity (open/non-manifold edges), degenerate triangles, duplicate faces, inverted normals (declared vs winding order), and binary file size consistency. Fixes the hidden failure mode in the provided validator that skips normal consistency checks.

    endloop

  endfacet

      vertex 0.0 0.0 0.0

endsolid repair_001

args.content:
import subprocess
import sys
import json

files = ['repair_001.stl', 'repair_002.stl', 'repair_003.stl']

for f in files:
    result = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, 'stl_normal_repair.py', f],
        capture_output=True, text=True
    )
    print(f'=== {f} ===')
    print('stdout:', result.stdout)
    print('stderr:', result.stderr)
    print('returncode:', result.returncode)
    print()

result.stdout:
